name: Capitol Bank
description_short: Capitol Bank is a community bank headquartered in Madison, Wisconsin, offering personal banking, business banking, and mortgage solutions with a focus on relationship-based service.
description_long:
Capitol Bank is a community-focused financial institution based in Madison, Wisconsin, with three physical branch locations in the greater Madison area (West Madison, Verona, and East Madison). The bank emphasizes local relationships and personalized service, with a team of 60 bankers involved in over 40 community boards and committees. The institution positions itself as a stable, trustworthy alternative to larger national banks, with direct contact and decision-making at the local level.

Capitol Bank offers three main service categories: personal banking (checking, savings, and other deposit products accessible through online and mobile platforms), business banking (including treasury management, commercial lending, and business checking/savings), and mortgage solutions (home purchase loans, refinancing, and mortgage consulting). The bank maintains separate online banking portals for personal and business customers, along with phone support and in-person branch services. Recent hires indicate expansion in commercial lending and treasury management services.

The bank distinguishes itself through explicit commitment to community investment, transparency, and relationship banking. Their marketing emphasizes personal banker knowledge, tailored financial solutions, open communication, and ethical principles (integrity, respect, diversity, and social/economic development). The website highlights their local roots and personal approach as differentiation from larger institutional competitors. Security awareness is also noted, with fraud alerts to customers about impersonation scams.

Capitol Bank appears to be a legitimate, established community bank with standard banking products and a traditional branch-based model. However, the website content provided is limited to marketing messages and general information; specific product details, fee structures, interest rates, lending terms, and regulatory information are not visible. As with any bank selection, consumers should review their specific product offerings, competitive rates, and FDIC insurance status before opening accounts. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking portal for personal customers
In-person branch banking at three locations
pros: Three physical branch locations in the Madison area for in-person banking and support
Personal banker relationships - 60-person team committed to understanding individual customer needs
Online and mobile banking platforms for both personal and business customers
Community-focused institution with 60+ bankers active on 40+ local community boards and committees
Dedicated business banking services including treasury management and commercial lending
Mortgage solutions with knowledgeable team for home purchase and refinance loans
Active fraud monitoring and customer alerts about impersonation scams targeting the bank
cons: Limited geographic footprint - only three branches in Madison area; not suitable for customers needing nationwide access
Website lacks specific product details, fee schedules, current rates, or loan terms required for rate/fee comparison
No information provided about FDIC insurance status, minimum deposit requirements, or regulatory oversight
No mention of digital-only banking options or extended hours beyond standard branch operations
Limited transparency on business lending criteria, commercial loan rates, or small business program specifics

contact.address: 710 N High Point Rd, Madison, WI 53717

best_for: Madison-area residents and businesses seeking personalized, local banking relationships
Small to mid-sized business owners needing commercial lending and treasury management services
Homebuyers in Wisconsin looking for local mortgage consultation and home loan origination
Customers who prioritize face-to-face banker relationships over digital-only banking
diagnosis: Capitol Bank is best for Madison-area residents and businesses seeking traditional community banking with personalized service and local decision-making. The main caveat is that specific product terms, rates, fees, and full service details are not disclosed on their website, requiring direct contact with the bank for comparative evaluation.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
